# Pr 20311 Safelock Fastpath Precision Toggle Kv Eviction

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_426ca69578a84dd4af512e5a2782086e
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
Introduce "safelock" fast-path gating + precision toggle and KV-eviction safety for runtime

Context and motivation:\nThis PR addresses a recurring class of incidents where aggressive fast-path kernel selection and lower-precision execution (fp16 / int8) produced rare but hard-to-detect correctness deviations for edge prompts. At the same time, customers expect throughput and lower cost. We need a runtime mechanism that lets us enable high-performance paths without risking silent correctness regressions in production.\n\nWhat this PR does (summary):\n- Adds a 'safelock' gating layer in the kernel selection path. The safelock prevents switching to a fast path unless a set of runtime validators pass (numerical consistency, kv-cache integrity checks, and recent success window).\n- Introduces a precision toggle API (per-model, per-deployment) with three modes: strict (fp32 only), balanced (fp16 with strict validators), aggressive (fp16/int8 allowed once warmed).\n- Adds a lightweight runtime validator harness: stochastic correctness probes run as background requests against canary inputs to compute divergence metrics (token-level mismatch rate, logit-delta distribution). Validators are cheap and attach metadata to requests that triggered path selection failures.\n- Changes KV cache eviction policy to be conservative when cache-warmness is below threshold; combined with safelock it avoids invalidating cached KV frames that fast kernels rely on.\n- Integrates safelock signals with the continuous-batching scheduler: until a deployment's validators reach a success threshold, scheduler prefers conservative batching and disables cross-model micro-batching that can expose interleaving bugs.\n- Adds observability hooks: metrics (safelock.blocked_count, validator.mismatch_rate, precision.switch_count, kv.eviction_suppressed) and a dashboard stub.\n- Adds a feature flag runtime.enable_safelock (default true for new deploys) and model config fields precision_mode and validator_policy.\n\nDesign notes and rationale:\n- The safelock is intentionally conservative: it is designed to minimize customer-visible regressions at the cost of initial throughput until a model warms. We prefer false negatives (staying on slow path) over false positives (silent incorrect outputs).\n- Validators are probabilistic and run only on a small fraction of traffic (configurable). We do not rely on end-user prompts alone; the system feeds a small canary corpus that includes known edge cases and adversarial tokens used in previous incidents.\n- KV-cache eviction suppression is implemented as an additional guard in the eviction worker: if cache_warmness < 0.7 and safelock.enabled, eviction respects a soft TTL and only evicts when memory pressure exceeds a higher threshold. This prevents state churn that invalidates prefix caching assumptions used by some kernels.\n\nCommits (high-level):\n- feat(runtime): add safelock kernel gating and validator harness (A. Kumar)\n- feat(config): model precision_mode and validator_policy proto + defaulting (A. Kumar)\n- fix(kv): conservative eviction path and warmness estimator (R. Patel)\n- perf(scheduler): integrate safelock signal into batching decisioning (L. O'Connor)\n- test: end-to-end validator integration tests and canary job (E. Tan)\n- docs: runtime safelock/precision-mode doc, migration notes (doc team)\n\nFiles touched (high-level):\n- runtime/kernel_selector.cpp/h (safelock implementation, gating checks)\n- runtime/precision_policy.proto (new model-level config)\n- runtime/validator/ (background probe harness, small canary corpus)\n- serving/scheduler/continuous_batcher.cpp (respect safelock signals)\n- serving/kvcache/eviction_worker.cpp (suppressed eviction path)\n- metrics/registry (new metrics added)\n- tests/e2e/validators_integration_test.cc (new suite)\n- docs/runtime/safelock.md (operator-facing guide and flags)\n\nSmall illustrative snippet included in the patch (non-executable):\n\"\"\"\nif (safelock.enabled() && !validators.passed_recently(dep)) {\n  // force fallback to conservative kernel + fp32 path\n  selectKernel = KernelSelector::conservative(model, seq_len);\n} else {\n  selectKernel = KernelSelector::fastpath(model, seq_len, precision_mode);\n}\n\"\"\"\n\nBenchmarks / numbers:\n- Baseline (main, before PR) on rtx-a100-80gb, long-tail tests: median latency 48ms, p99 180ms, throughput 2200 tok/s.\n- With safelock enabled (default config) immediately after deploy: median latency +6% (51ms), throughput -8% (2024 tok/s) while validators warm.\n- After 30m of live traffic and validator success: median latency +1% (48.5ms), throughput -2% (2160 tok/s).\n- Aggressive mode (opt-in, warm): throughput +12% over baseline; we see a measurable cost win.\n- We ran a correctness sweep against our regression corpus (n=1200 prompts) showing token-level mismatch reduced to <0.02% for balanced mode; in aggressive mode mismatch rises to ~0.6% on the corpus (expected, opt-in).\n\nTesting and CI:\n- Added unit + integration tests for validator harness and eviction behavior.\n- Added synthetic stochastic tests that simulate rare floating-point edgecases.\n- CI: initial pipeline had one flaky test (eviction_worker race) failing on Linux-asan; fixed in second commit. Final CI: all checks passed (build, unit, integration, perf-smoke).\n\nReview discussion (highlights):\n- Marco: \"I like the safelock abstraction; can we ensure the validator corpus is editable by SREs without code changes?\"\n- Aisha: \"Yes — validators read a canary file from /etc/redwood/validators/corpus.json and we added operator docs. SRE can update via configmap for k8s deploys.\"\n- Eve: \"Concerned about metric cardinality when we annotate every request with validator metadata.\"\n- Aisha: \"We emit aggregated counters and histograms; only a small sampling logs request-level debug traces behind a flag.\"\n- Liam: \"Scheduler changes look sane. Need additional canary rollout plan for enterprise customers with strict SLAs.\"\n- Aisha: \"Added rollout doc and an opt-out label on deployment config; default for existing deployments is unchanged (safe-mode learned).\"\n\nEdge cases / known tradeoffs:\n- There is an initial throughput hit until validators warm. We mitigate by sampling only a small portion of traffic for validators and allowing manual warm-up (canary traffic injection).\n- Aggressive precision_mode is opt-in; customers should enable only after verifying on internal test prompts. We include a CLI tool for running the validator corpus locally against a deployment.\n- The KV eviction suppression increases memory use under some failure modes; we documented emergency knobs and introduced an automatic disable threshold if memory pressure remains high for 10s.\n\nMigration / operator guidance:\n- New deploys: safelock enabled by default. Operators can set runtime.enable_safelock=false to revert (not recommended).\n- To opt into aggressive precision: update model config: precision_mode=aggressive and monitor validator.mismatch_rate.\n- Dashboards: added a starter Grafana dashboard (docs link) and alerts for validator.mismatch_rate > 0.1% and safelock.blocked_count being unexpectedly high.\n\nBackward compatibility:\n- All config fields default to conservative behavior; no breaking changes. Models without precision_mode set continue to use fp32 until they pass validators.\n\nPost-merge actions / follow-ups (linear):\n- ENG-4983: add automated warmup job to inject synthetic canary traffic during deploy windows (follow-up).\n- ENG-5122: add per-customer precision analytics and billing impact report.\n\nMerge outcome:\n- Final status: merged via squash. Branch merged to main after approvals from Marco, Eve, and Liam. CI green at merge.\n\nIf you want to test locally: run tools/runtime_cli --run-validators --config test/configs/precision_local.yaml against a dev deployment; run scripts/kv_warmup.sh to pre-fill the kvcache with the canary prefix set.\n\nThanks to Rohan Patel for eviction work, and the QA team for extended regression runs.\n\nPatch size: moderate; favors safety and observability over aggressive micro-optimizations up front.\n
Added a runtime 'safelock' gating mechanism to avoid correctness regressions when enabling fast-path kernels and lower-precision execution. New precision toggle with runtime validators. KV-cache eviction made conservative under uncertain cache-warmness. Backwards-compatible; opt-in via config.
